Title: Dissecting the anti-obesity components of ginseng: How ginseng polysaccharides and ginsenosides target gut microbiota to suppress high-fat diet-induced obesity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Short-chain fatty acids; glucose (via intestinal gluconeogenesis)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite suppression and anti-obesity via gut microbiota-mediated SCFA-GLP-1/PYY signaling and IGN activation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with degradation of ginseng polysaccharide pectin/galactose-containing glycans and linked to anti-obesity microbiota remodeling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH35 was enriched in the GP-treated microbiome. 2. GH35 is predicted to degrade galactose-containing residues in heterogeneous pectins of ginseng polysaccharides. 3. GH35-encoding GP-enriched bacteria were associated with downstream SCFA/IGN-related microbial functions that ameliorated HFD-induced obesity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected/enriched in fecal metagenome of GP-treated HFD-fed mice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation against CAZy; differential abundance analysis; species-enzyme association from metagenome data
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; oral ginseng polysaccharide administration
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome engineering target for enriching GP-utilizing bacteria in anti-obesity functional foods/probiotics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causality not validated by isolated gene manipulation; enzyme abundance inferred from metagenome rather than direct expression/activity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study experimentally centers on microbial functional genes/orthologs rather than host genes; GH35 is treated here as a core microbiome metabolism gene because it is explicitly highlighted in Results/Conclusion.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Short-chain fatty acids; glucose (via intestinal gluconeogenesis)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite suppression and anti-obesity via gut microbiota-mediated SCFA-GLP-1/PYY signaling and IGN activation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with degradation of arabinose-containing pectic glycans in GP and with GP-specific microbiota remodeling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH43 family members were enriched by GP treatment. 2. GH43 contains arabinanase/arabinofuranosidase activities predicted to digest ara residues in GP pectins. 3. Enrichment of GH43 aligns with selective expansion of GP-associated gut bacteria and anti-obesity metabolic output.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected/enriched in fecal metagenome of GP-treated HFD-fed mice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation against CAZy; differential abundance analysis; species-enzyme association
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; oral ginseng polysaccharide administration
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate microbial enzyme marker to evaluate GP-responsive anti-obesity microbiota
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Family-level assignment may mask isoform-specific function; direct biochemical validation not performed in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also mentions GH43_29 and GH43_33 as GP-enriched subfamilies, but GH43 is the main highlighted family in the abstract.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Short-chain fatty acids; glucose (via intestinal gluconeogenesis)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite suppression and anti-obesity via gut microbiota-mediated SCFA-GLP-1/PYY signaling and IGN activation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with pectin degradation in GP and GP-specific microbial restructuring linked to obesity suppression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PL9_1 was enriched in the GP-treated microbiome. 2. PL9_1 includes pectate lyase activity that cleaves pectins in ginseng polysaccharides. 3. Clostridiales bacterium encoding PL9_1 was highlighted as a core GP-associated species.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAZyme polysaccharide lyase family 9 subfamily 1; pectate lyase-related
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected/enriched in fecal metagenome of GP-treated HFD-fed mice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation against CAZy; differential abundance analysis; species-enzyme association; co-occurrence network
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; oral ginseng polysaccharide administration
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker for selecting GP-responsive bacterial consortia in obesity interventions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct enzyme assay in isolated strain reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly stated in abstract and conclusion as a representative GP-targeted CAZyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Short-chain fatty acids; glucose (via intestinal gluconeogenesis)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite suppression and anti-obesity via gut microbiota-mediated SCFA-GLP-1/PYY signaling and IGN activation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with degradation of glycosidic linkages in ginsenosides and GS-specific microbiota remodeling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH16_5 was selectively enriched by GS treatment. 2. GH16_5 was inferred to have beta-glycosidase activity capable of degrading glycosidic linkages between aglycone and glycosyls in ginsenosides. 3. GH16_5-encoding S. halorespirans was highlighted as a GS-enriched species linked to anti-obesity efficacy.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAZyme glycoside hydrolase family 16 subfamily 5; beta-glycosidase-related
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected/enriched in fecal metagenome of GS-treated HFD-fed mice
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic annotation against CAZy; differential abundance analysis; species-enzyme association
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome engineering marker for GS-responsive anti-obesity interventions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic activity inferred from CAZy/literature rather than directly assayed in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly highlighted in abstract, Results, and Conclusion as a key GS-targeted CAZyme.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacteroides stercorirosoris; Clostridiales bacterium; Eubacterium sp. 14-2 and other GP-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite regulation and anti-obesity through SCFA signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GP upregulated K00625, promoting acetyl-CoA/acetate-related microbial metabolism associated with obesity suppression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00625 was significantly enriched by GP but not emphasized for GS. 2. GP-enriched bacteria encoded more K00625 than GS-enriched bacteria. 3. Increased fecal acetyl-CoA and stronger acetate-enrichment tendency were consistent with K00625-based prediction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GP-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GP-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to phosphate to form acetyl phosphate in the acetate-forming pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; differential abundance analysis; metabolite profiling of acetyl-CoA/SCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbial functional marker for GP-responsive acetate-producing consortia
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: KO-based assignment does not prove transcript/protein activity in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper describes K00625 as related to generation of acetyl-CoA and acetate/propionate production context; exact strain-level catalytic flux was inferred from annotation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacteroides stercorirosoris; Clostridiales bacterium; Eubacterium sp. 14-2 and other GP-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite regulation and anti-obesity through SCFA signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GP upregulated K00925 and favored acetate/propionate-producing microbial functions associated with reduced adiposity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00925 was significantly enriched by GP treatment. 2. GP-enriched bacteria encoded more K00925 than GS-enriched bacteria. 3. Species positively correlated with K00925 included B. stercorirosoris, Ruminococcus bromii and Coprococcus catus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GP-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GP-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Microbial SCFA metabolism / acetate and propionate-associated pathways
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms acetate and supports SCFA output
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts acetyl phosphate to acetate with ATP generation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; differential abundance analysis; correlation with bacterial species; SCFA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for GP-induced acetate-producing functionality in microbiome-directed obesity management
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional annotation only; direct enzymatic validation lacking
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text links K00925 to acetate and propionate production context in GP-treated microbiome.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: succinate-semialdehyde dehydrogenase / succinate-related enzyme (KO assignment as reported)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Candidatus Gastranaerophilales bacterium HUM_1; GS-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Succinate; short-chain fatty acid-related metabolic output
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-obesity and metabolic regulation, including thermogenesis-related benefits discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS enriched K18118, associated with increased succinate production and IGN-related node activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K18118 was significantly enriched by GS treatment. 2. The paper links K18118 to succinate synthesis in SCFA metabolism and also to IGN-related reactions. 3. GS-treated mice showed stronger succinate accumulation than GP-treated mice.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GS-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GS-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Microbial SCFA metabolism; intestinal gluconeogenesis-related substrate supply
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms or supports succinate pool that contributes to anti-obesity signaling and IGN
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Annotated in the paper as participating in succinate synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; differential abundance analysis; correlation with bacteria; fecal succinate measurement
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for GS-responsive succinate-producing microbiota with anti-obesity potential
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact enzyme identity/reaction for this KO was not fully expanded in the provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This KO was also discussed in relation to IGN, indicating dual relevance in the study’s mechanism model.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase / upstream butyrate-pathway enzyme (as pathway assignment reported)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Pseudoflavonifractor sp. Marseille-P3106; uncultured Roseburia sp.; GS-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite regulation, anti-inflammatory effects, and anti-obesity benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS enriched K00100, indicating stronger microbial capacity in upstream butyrate synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00100 was significantly enriched by GS treatment. 2. GS-enriched species including Pseudoflavonifractor sp. Marseille-P3106 were predicted to encode K00100. 3. The paper places K00100 upstream of butyrate synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GS-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GS-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Annotated by the paper as an upstream step in the butyrate synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; correlation with bacteria; fecal butyrate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for selecting GS-responsive butyrogenic bacterial consortia
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Butyrate ultimately increased more clearly in GP than GS, indicating pathway output depends on additional nodes and precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KO-to-enzyme-name mapping is generalized here because the manuscript emphasizes KO function in pathway context more than formal enzyme nomenclature.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: butyrate synthesis-related enzyme (upstream KO as reported)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Pseudoflavonifractor sp. Marseille-P3106; uncultured Roseburia sp.; GS-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Appetite regulation, anti-inflammatory effects, and anti-obesity benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS enriched K18122, supporting a GS-specific upstream butyrate-producing module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K18122 was significantly enriched by GS treatment. 2. GS-enriched species encoded K18122 more abundantly than GP-enriched species. 3. K18122 was used to explain the GS-associated butyrate synthesis potential.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GS-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GS-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Annotated by the paper as an upstream reaction in the butyrate synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; bacterial association; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful functional marker for GS-shaped butyrate-related microbial metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Predicted pathway capacity did not fully match measured butyrate output, suggesting additional regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The manuscript uses K18122 primarily as a KO-level pathway node rather than a fully named enzyme target.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacteroides stercorirosoris; Clostridiales bacterium; Flavonifractor sp. An10 and other GP-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy homeostasis, reduced energy intake, improved glucose metabolism, anti-obesity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GP enriched K01678 and supported stronger IGN activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K01678 was significantly increased by GP treatment. 2. The paper assigns K01678 to the IGN-related conversion of fumarate to (S)-malate. 3. GP-enriched species were positively correlated with K01678 and GP produced stronger PEPCK activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GP-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GP-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream precursor for IGN glucose production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fumarate to malate in the IGN-related sequence described in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; bacterial association; biochemical assays of PEPCK/G6Pase/G6P/glucose
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for GP-responsive microbiome functions that enhance IGN and glucose homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Microbial KO enrichment is indirect evidence for host IGN activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IGN activation was validated biochemically at pathway level rather than through direct manipulation of this microbial gene.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacteroides stercorirosoris; Clostridiales bacterium; Flavonifractor sp. An10 and other GP-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy homeostasis, reduced energy intake, improved glucose metabolism, anti-obesity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GP enriched K00024, contributing to OAA formation in IGN-related pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00024 was significantly increased by GP treatment. 2. The paper assigns K00024 to malate-to-oxaloacetate conversion in IGN. 3. GP-enriched species positively correlated with K00024.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GP-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GP-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for PEP and downstream glucose production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes malate to oxaloacetate in the IGN-related reaction chain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; bacterial association; biochemical IGN assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Functional marker for GP-induced IGN-supportive microbiota
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The manuscript treats this as a GP-specific IGN node at the microbiome functional level.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Bacteroides stercorirosoris; Clostridiales bacterium; Flavonifractor sp. An10 and other GP-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy homeostasis, reduced energy intake, improved glucose metabolism, anti-obesity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GP enriched K01596 and was associated with stronger PEPCK activity and higher glucose production in IGN
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K01596 was significantly increased by GP treatment. 2. The paper assigns K01596 to oxaloacetate-to-PEP conversion in IGN. 3. Biochemical data showed stronger PEPCK activity in GP than GS.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GP-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GP-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms key precursor for glucose production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts oxaloacetate to phosphoenolpyruvate in the IGN-related pathway described by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported at host pathway level; microbial KO-level rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; biochemical assay of PEPCK activity; glucose/G6P measurements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome functional marker for boosting IGN-associated anti-obesity benefit of GP
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Microbial KO enrichment and host PEPCK activity are mechanistically connected but not gene-causally separated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Discussion explicitly notes PEPCK as an important rate-limiting enzyme in gluconeogenesis.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: aspartate aminotransferase / L-aspartate to oxaloacetate enzyme (as reported)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Candidatus Gastranaerophilales bacterium HUM_1; GS-enriched gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy homeostasis, reduced energy intake, improved glucose metabolism, anti-obesity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GS enriched K00278, indicating a GS-specific entry point into IGN through OAA formation from L-aspartate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K00278 was notably increased in the GS group. 2. The paper assigns K00278 to transformation of L-aspartate into oxaloacetate in IGN. 3. GS-enriched Candidatus Gastranaerophilales bacterium HUM_1 encoded K00278 and positively correlated with it.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher encoded abundance in GS-enriched species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher cumulative TPM in fecal metagenome of GS-treated mice
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies oxaloacetate precursor for downstream glucose formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to conversion of L-aspartate to oxaloacetate in the IGN-related pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomics; KEGG KO annotation; bacterial correlation; biochemical IGN assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for GS-activated IGN-supporting microbiome function
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact enzyme identity is not explicitly detailed in manuscript text provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GS and GP activated IGN through different KO nodes, with K00278 being a GS-specific one in this study.
